---
title: "Mete Akcaoglu"
---

::: {.hero}
![](akcaoglu_22_full.jpg){.hero-photo}

:::: {.hero-text}
[Professor of Instructional Technology · Department Chair]{.hero-lead}

I am a professor of instructional technology and department chair at Georgia
Southern University. My research focuses on how people learn with and through
technology with particular emphasis on motivation, interest development, and the design of technology-rich learning environments. I study game-based learning, interest development, and AI in educational settings. My work spans experimental, survey, and qualitative methods. 

::: {.social-links}
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=0y1b0oEAAAAJ&hl=en) · [GitHub](https://github.com/meteakca) · [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/meteakcaoglu/) · [Bluesky](https://bsky.app/profile/meteakca.bsky.social)
:::
::::
:::

## Research Interests

::: {.interest-cards}
:::: {.interest-card}
[Motivation & Interest Development]{.interest-card-title}

How interest emerge in technology-rich environments, and how instructional design can sustain them. [→ Related work](research.html)
::::

:::: {.interest-card}
[Game-Based Learning & CS Education]{.interest-card-title}

Using game design as a vehicle for teaching computational thinking in K–12 classrooms. [→ Project GAME](projects.html)
::::

:::: {.interest-card}
[AI in Education]{.interest-card-title}

LLM-assisted research workflows, AI literacy, and the role of generative AI in teaching and learning.
::::

:::: {.interest-card}
[Online Learning Design]{.interest-card-title}

Community of inquiry frameworks, course design patterns, and engagement in online and hybrid settings.
::::
:::

## Current Position

**Department Chair — Leadership, Technology, and Human Development**
Georgia Southern University · Fall 2024–Present

Overseeing 20 full-time faculty, 10 graduate programs, ~600 students. Focus
areas: faculty development, curriculum reform, enrollment management, and
accreditation.

## News

::: {.news-feed}
2026
: Co-authored *Computational Analysis of Educational Data: A Field Guide Using R* with Wang, Rosenberg, & Kellogg. [Read online →](https://data-edu.github.io/computational-social-science-cookbook/)

2025–26
: Co-PI on Math Nation Georgia Pilot Program Evaluation ($1M, Georgia Department of Education)

2024
: Keynote at the International Symposium on Computer and Instructional Technologies (ICITS), Kastamonu, Türkiye

2024
: Appointed Department Chair, Leadership, Technology, and Human Development

2022
: Completed NSF-funded Project GAME: a game design-based CS curriculum for middle schools
:::
